title: vicia ferruginea
intro: Vicia ferruginea, also known as the rusty broomrape, is a flowering plant species in the borage family that is native to Europe, western Asia, and north Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory problems, and aiding in digestion. Vicia ferruginea can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effectiveness.
